title: Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A hearty, aromatic stew originating from Morocco, slow-cooked in a clay pot called a tagine. This recipe combines the rich flavors of lamb, spices, and dried fruits.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: Bone-in or boneless, cut into 2-inch pieces

ingredients.name: Moroccan spice blend
ingredients.notes: Available at most Middle Eastern markets or online

instructions: 1. Heat the olive oil in the tagine pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
2. Brown the lamb pieces on all sides, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the Moroccan spice blend and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
4. Add the water and bring to a boil.
5. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
6. Add the dried apricots and prunes, and continue to simmer for another 30 minutes.
7. Fluff the couscous and serve alongside the lamb and sauce.
8. Deglaze the pot with a little water if the sauce is too thick.
9. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
